evidence: The docstring's statement — linear forms a_i*n+b_i with no fixed prime divisor of the product are simultaneously prime for infinitely many n — is precisely Dickson's conjecture on prime values of linear forms.
The module header titles the problem the 'Prime Tuples Conjecture', asserting infinitude of prime k-tuples of the linear forms a_i*n+b_i.
Reed's conjecture bounds the chromatic number chi(G) by ceil((omega(G)+Delta(G)+1)/2) per the docstring.
The docstring defines omega(G) as 'the size of the largest clique subgraph', i.e. the clique number, one of the two bounding quantities in Reed's conjecture.
The finite-graph variant states the same Reed bound chi(G) <= ceil((omega(G)+Delta(G)+1)/2) on the chromatic number.
The docstring says 'This problem's name comes from the fact that it is a generalization of Prince Rupert's Cube'.
The question 'are all convex polyhedra with nonempty interior Rupert?' quantifies over convex polyhedra given as convex hulls of finite vertex sets.
The docstring states the conjecture strengthens Huang's sensitivity theorem, which 'gives a quartic bound, bs(f) <= s(f)^4, thereby settling the original conjecture', to a quadratic bound.
The conjecture is stated 'for every Boolean function f : {0,1}^n -> {0,1}', bounding its block sensitivity by its sensitivity squared.
The classical Voronovskaja asymptotic formula concerns the Bernstein operators built from Bernstein basis polynomials p_{n,j}(x) = C(n,j) x^j (1-x)^(n-j).
The open problem asks for the Voronovskaja-type asymptotics of the Bezier variant B_{n,alpha} of the Bernstein operators, defined via powers of tails of Bernstein polynomials.
The module header points to 'Fuglede's spectral set conjecture, which motivates the study of weak tilings' for these problems from arXiv:2506.23631.
Problem 3 in [Ar2013] asks for a ZFC example of a weakly first countable compact Hausdorff space 'which is not first countable'.
Problem 2 in [Ar2013] asks for a weakly first-countable compact Hausdorff space X whose cardinality exceeds the continuum (c < |X|).
The docstring's blanket convention makes the sought space compact Hausdorff (CompactSpace X and T2Space X in the statement).
The docstring says 'Under CH, such a space ... exists as constructed in [Ya1976] by Yakovlev', with the hypothesis Fact (aleph-1 = c).
Zagier's conjecture [Za94] concerns the Q-dimension of the space spanned by multiple zeta values zeta(s_1,...,s_k) of weight n.
The solved upper bound [Te02, DG05] states the dimension of the Q-vector space of multiple zeta values of weight n is at most d_n.
